title: build, commit: add support for disabling image history with `--omit-history`
body:
As per `OCI` `image-spec` setting history is optional so buildah must
support these use-cases via `--omit-history=true` some of the use-cases
are discussed below.

* There are use-cases when image-spec contributes to the image size by
  adding build history to image-spec in certain use-cases this image
history is not required by the end-users but there is no way to remove
it. See: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3513

* Certain build tools don't honor setting build history in image spec
  however buildah does not allow processing images without build history
since setting build history is still optional as per `OCI` image-spec
( https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/config.md#properties)
so buildah must support such use-cases.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4025
Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3513

comments.body: @nalind PTAL this should close both the use-cases of working with build tools which ignores setting build history and also disabling build history for cases where users don't need it.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Is this supposed to be preventing the builder from adding history entries to those inherited from the base image, or clearing them as well?  The docs suggest one thing, but the test expects the other, and I don't know which one is right.

> Is this supposed to be preventing the builder from adding history entries to those inherited from the base image, or clearing them as well? The docs suggest one thing, but the test expects the other, and I don't know which one is right.

Updated the docs with the suggestions that should fix it and makes it more clear i.e `--add-history=false` would mean to omit the optional `History` field from `image-spec`.
Added another commit to remove stutter from `--identity-label` and `--add-history` itself.
I personally still think that a defaults-to-false "--omit-history" is easier to explain, but this works.

> I personally still think that a defaults-to-false "--omit-history" is easier to explain, but this works.

@nalind We can still flip it no rush, just tried to keep it what was discussed here https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3513#issuecomment-975899755 since `config` has a similar option. But we can still flip its a quick change.
In `buildah config` it affects the change being made for that command, and defaults to false, while this similarly-named option has the opposite default, which can get confusing.

If I'm reading the logic right, if we start with a base image that has no history but has layers, we create a new image that has one history entry in it and multiple layers, having bypassed the logic that tries to verify that the number of non-"empty-layer" history entries in the image we're about to create matches the number of layers the new image will have.  If we then try to use that new image as a base image for another build, that logic won't be bypassed by default because that new image has some history information, and we'll get an error.

Okay, that makes sense.  Really not liking that it effectively imposes a requirement that the new option has be used, basically always, if one of these images was used as a base at any point in an image's ancestry, but since we need to produce images that look like what docker build produces, it sounds like we're going to be stuck with it.  Patch looks good.
